name: Shannon
description: Autonomous white-box AI pentester by Keygraph that reads your source code, identifies attack vectors, and executes real exploits in a sandbox to prove vulnerabilities rather than just report theoretical ones.

last_release.summary: Proof-by-exploitation AI pentester with multi-agent architecture across reconnaissance, vulnerability analysis, and exploitation phases

pricing.free_tier_limits: Shannon Lite — AGPL-3.0, unlimited self-hosted scans for sandbox/staging environments
pricing.high_water_mark_reason: Freemium with free tier; no peak-lock evidence

lock_in.data_portability: Full — all reports are local markdown + JSON; no vendor data lock.
lock_in.explanation: Open-source AGPL Lite edition, self-hosted, runs in your Docker. Pro tier adds features but Lite remains functional standalone.
lock_in.reason: OSS, self-hosted, report artifacts are plain files

transparency.reason: GitHub public, AGPL Lite tier free, but Pro pricing is 'contact sales'

developer_experience.reason: One-line npx setup, Claude Agent SDK, CI integration via GitHub Actions/GitLab/Jenkins

when_to_use: Catching OWASP-critical bugs (SQLi, XSS, SSRF, broken auth) before prod in staging pipelines
Validating fixes by re-running exploits on patched branches
Supplementing human pentesters with continuous white-box coverage
Monorepo security coverage via Docker + Claude Agent SDK
CI/CD security gate with proof-by-exploitation rather than noisy DAST alerts
when_not_to_use: Production live traffic — Shannon runs real exploits that can mutate data
Black-box third-party apps where you don't own the source code
Compliance-only needs requiring certified auditors, not automated tools
Air-gapped environments without access to Anthropic API (or AWS Bedrock/Vertex)

pricing_at_scale.estimate: Free via Shannon Lite (AGPL) — you pay Anthropic API usage only, ~$5-15 per full scan of a medium codebase
Lite: ~$100-300/mo API cost. Pro: contact sales (typically mid-four-figure annual)

pricing_at_scale.scale: Enterprise monorepo with multiple daily scans
pricing_at_scale.estimate: Pro tier with SAST/SCA/pentest bundle — enterprise annual contract

pros: Proof-by-exploitation means zero false positives in the final report — every bug comes with a working PoC
96.15% on the XBOW benchmark (100 of 104 exploit challenges), substantially higher than most commercial DAST
Multi-agent architecture runs reconnaissance + parallel vuln analysis + exploitation concurrently
Full scans in 1-1.5 hours, with CI integration and 2FA/TOTP authentication support
cons: Only safe in sandbox/staging — exploits can mutate data, create users, trigger side effects
Requires your Anthropic API key (or AWS Bedrock / Vertex AI) — ongoing token cost per scan
White-box only: source code access required, cannot test third-party SaaS you don't own
LLM-generated findings occasionally need human validation despite the exploit verification step
review.verdict: If you ship web apps you can point Shannon at a staging copy of, the proof-by-exploitation model is a meaningful step up from noisy DAST: you get fewer, more actionable findings with reproducible PoCs. Don't run it against prod.
review.best_for: Engineering teams with a staging environment who want continuous OWASP coverage without paying for human pentests every sprint
review.not_for: Black-box testing scenarios, production environments, or teams with zero tolerance for AI-in-the-loop security decisions

seo.title: Shannon Review 2026 — AI Pentester Powered by Claude Agent SDK
seo.meta_description: Shannon is an autonomous white-box AI pentester by Keygraph: reads your source code, runs real exploits, and proves OWASP vulnerabilities with 96% XBOW benchmark accuracy.
